---
title: "02 — Data Profiling & Quality"
description: |
  Assesses whether the MedStar charge data is complete and internally
  consistent enough to support downstream cost modeling.
dependencies:
  - data/intermediate/medstar_charges.parquet
  - data/intermediate/episode_codes.parquet
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

## Load Data

```{python}
import polars as pl
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from _common import INTERMEDIATE_DIR, CHARGE_COLS, CPT_DESCRIPTIONS

medstar = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "medstar_charges.parquet")
episode_codes = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "episode_codes.parquet")
print(f"MedStar rows: {medstar.shape[0]:,}")
```

## Charge Column Completeness

How much of each charge column is actually populated? Null values mean the
hospital didn't report that charge type; zeros may indicate free services
or data entry gaps.

```{python}
total = medstar.shape[0]
stats_rows = []

for col in CHARGE_COLS:
    series = medstar[col]
    null_count = series.null_count()
    numeric = series.drop_nulls().cast(pl.Float64, strict=False).drop_nulls()
    zero_count = (numeric == 0).sum()
    stats_rows.append({
        "column": col,
        "null_rate": round(null_count / total, 3),
        "zero_rate": round(zero_count / total, 3) if len(numeric) > 0 else None,
    })

cols = [r["column"] for r in stats_rows]
null_rates = [r["null_rate"] for r in stats_rows]
zero_rates = [r["zero_rate"] or 0 for r in stats_rows]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    y=cols, x=null_rates, orientation="h", name="Null Rate",
    marker_color="#d32f2f",
    hovertemplate="%{y}<br>Null: %{x:.1%}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    y=cols, x=zero_rates, orientation="h", name="Zero Rate",
    marker_color="#ff9800",
    hovertemplate="%{y}<br>Zero: %{x:.1%}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    title="Charge Column Completeness",
    xaxis_title="Rate",
    xaxis=dict(tickformat=".0%"),
    height=300,
    margin=dict(l=140),
)
fig.show()
```

## Code Coverage

Do we have charge data for all 21 episode CPT codes? Each dot represents
one code — green if found in at least one hospital's data, red if absent
entirely.

```{python}
codes_in_data = set(medstar["cpt_code"].unique().to_list())
codes_expected = episode_codes.sort("clinical_role", "cpt_code")

statuses = []
for row in codes_expected.iter_rows(named=True):
    code = row["cpt_code"]
    statuses.append({
        "cpt_code": code,
        "clinical_role": row["clinical_role"],
        "found": code in codes_in_data,
        "desc": CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(code, ""),
    })

found_count = sum(1 for s in statuses if s["found"])
labels = [s["cpt_code"] for s in statuses]
colors = ["#388e3c" if s["found"] else "#d32f2f" for s in statuses]
descs = [s["desc"] for s in statuses]
roles = [s["clinical_role"] for s in statuses]

fig = go.Figure(go.Scatter(
    x=list(range(len(labels))),
    y=[1] * len(labels),
    mode="markers",
    marker=dict(size=18, color=colors, symbol="circle"),
    text=labels,
    customdata=list(zip(descs, roles, ["Found" if s["found"] else "Missing" for s in statuses])),
    hovertemplate="<b>CPT %{text}</b><br>%{customdata[0]}<br>Role: %{customdata[1]}<br>%{customdata[2]}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.update_layout(
    title=f"Episode Code Coverage: {found_count}/{len(statuses)} found",
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=list(range(len(labels))), ticktext=labels, tickangle=-45, tickfont=dict(size=8)),
    yaxis=dict(visible=False),
    height=200,
    margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=20, r=20),
    showlegend=False,
)
fig.show()
```

## Structural Anomalies

Checks for rows where charge values are internally contradictory — these
indicate data entry errors or reporting inconsistencies.

```{python}
has_min_max = medstar.filter(
    pl.col("charge_min").is_not_null() & pl.col("charge_max").is_not_null()
)
min_gt_max = has_min_max.filter(
    pl.col("charge_min").cast(pl.Float64) > pl.col("charge_max").cast(pl.Float64)
)

has_gross_disc = medstar.filter(
    pl.col("charge_gross").is_not_null() & pl.col("charge_discounted").is_not_null()
)
gross_lt_disc = has_gross_disc.filter(
    pl.col("charge_gross").cast(pl.Float64) < pl.col("charge_discounted").cast(pl.Float64)
)

anomalies = [
    {"type": "min > max", "count": min_gt_max.shape[0], "checked": has_min_max.shape[0]},
    {"type": "gross < discounted", "count": gross_lt_disc.shape[0], "checked": has_gross_disc.shape[0]},
]
anomaly_df = pl.DataFrame(anomalies)

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    y=[a["type"] for a in anomalies],
    x=[a["count"] for a in anomalies],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color="#d32f2f",
    customdata=[a["checked"] for a in anomalies],
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>%{x} of %{customdata} rows<extra></extra>",
))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Structural Anomalies",
    xaxis_title="Row Count",
    height=200,
    margin=dict(l=180, t=40, b=30),
)
fig.show()
```

## Verdict

```{python}
found = sum(1 for s in statuses if s["found"])
missing = sum(1 for s in statuses if not s["found"])
total_anomalies = sum(a["count"] for a in anomalies)
worst_null = max(stats_rows, key=lambda r: r["null_rate"])

print("DATA QUALITY VERDICT")
print()
print(f"  Completeness: {worst_null['column']} has the highest null rate "
      f"at {worst_null['null_rate']:.1%}")
print(f"  Code coverage: {found}/{found + missing} episode codes present")
print(f"  Anomalies: {total_anomalies} structurally inconsistent rows")
print()
if worst_null["null_rate"] > 0.5:
    print("  ⚠ High null rates in one or more charge columns — downstream")
    print("    analysis should prefer columns with better coverage.")
if missing > 0:
    missing_codes = [s["cpt_code"] for s in statuses if not s["found"]]
    print(f"  ⚠ Missing codes: {', '.join(missing_codes)}")
    print("    Episode cost estimates will be incomplete for these components.")
if total_anomalies == 0:
    print("  ✓ No structural anomalies detected.")
else:
    print(f"  ⚠ {total_anomalies} rows with contradictory values — minor impact")
    print("    on medians but worth noting.")
print()
print("  Proceed to variance analysis (03) with awareness of gaps above.")
```
